title: OpenWrt One – Open Hardware Router
titleZh: OpenWrt One – 开源硬件路由器
author: peter_d_sherman
relevanceReason: 命中 1 个 AI/科技关键词。
summaryRaw: OpenWrt One is based on the MediaTek Filogic 820 SoC and has WiFi 6, dual-band, 3×3/2×2, 1x 2.5Gbit WAN, 1x 1Gbit LAN, 1GB DDR4 RAM, 256 MiB NAND, 16 MiB NOR (for recovery), M.2 SSD, USB-C Serial console and USB 2.0. Power Over Ethernet (POE): an IEEE 802.3af/at compliant device can power the device via the RJ-45 2.5 Gbps WAN connector.

Each Openwrt One comes flashed with the most current OpenWrt Release Firmware available based on the current manufacturing batch date. LuCI GUI will be installed, and the device is ready to run Out Of the Box using standard default settings.
before powering up the device make sure the NAND/NOR switch is set to NAND
connect to the 1G port of the device via Ethernet through 192.168.1.1
power on the device and wait for the green led to go solid indicating the device has booted.
summaryZh: OpenWrt One is based on the MediaTek Filogic 820 SoC and has WiFi 6, dual-band, 3×3/2×2, 1x 2.5Gbit WAN, 1x 1Gbit LAN, 1GB DDR4 RAM, 256 MiB NAND, 16 MiB NOR (for recovery), M.2 SSD, USB-C Serial console and USB 2.0. Power Over Ethernet (POE): an IEEE 802.3af/at compliant device can power the device via the RJ-45 2.5 Gbps WAN connector.

Each Openwrt One comes flashed with the most current OpenWrt Release Firmware available based on the current manufacturing batch date. LuCI GUI will be installed, and the device is ready to run Out Of the Box using standard default settings.

comments.author: PaulKeeble
comments.textRawHtml:
They are working on an OpenWRT Two at the moment which will be Wifi 7.

OpenWRT runs on a lot of hardware and its a great way to extend the life of a router past the manufacturers patches as well as gain a lot of capabilities. I wouldn't buy a commercial router that wasn't supported by OpenWRT now.
comments.textZhHtml:
他们目前正在开发 OpenWRT Two，这将是 Wifi 7。

OpenWRT 在许多硬件上运行，这是延长路由器寿命（超越制造商补丁）并获得许多功能的好方法。我现在不会购买 OpenWRT 不支持的商用路由器。

children.author: WithinReason
children.textRawHtml:
The planned specs are here, they say it will be made by GL.iNet:

https://openwrt.org/voting/2025-02-12-openwrt-two

Otherwise this router from GL.iNet has OpenWRT preinstalled, Wifi 7, 5x2.5G:

http://www.gl-inet.com/en-gb/products/gl-be9300
children.textZhHtml:
计划的规格在这里，他们说它将由 GL.iNet 制作：

https://openwrt.org/voting/2025-02-12-openwrt-two

否则 GL.iNet 的这款路由器预装了 OpenWRT，Wifi 7、5x2.5G：

http://www.gl-inet.com/en-gb/products/gl-be9300

children.author: vsviridov
children.textRawHtml: Why do they always have to look like some unholy blend of a cybernetic spider and a Knight Rider? What happened to a plain unassuming looking piece of industrial hardware...
children.textZhHtml: 为什么他们总是看起来像控制论蜘蛛和骑士的邪恶混合体？一个看起来不起眼的工业硬件发生了什么......

children.author: Karliss
children.textRawHtml:
Wifi 5-7 happened, now operating at 3 different frequency ranges (2.4, 5 and 6Ghz) and using techniques like beam forming and MIMO. All those antennas need to go somewhere.

If you want plain unassuming looking hardware get dedicated wifi access points and place them all over the building. There are plenty of those shaped liked big smoke detectors.

If you want single device there are also quite a few trash can shaped home routers.
children.textZhHtml:
Wifi 5-7 已经出现，现在在 3 个不同的频率范围（2.4、5 和 6Ghz）下运行，并使用波束成形和 MIMO 等技术。所有这些天线都需要安装到某个地方。

如果您想要外观朴素、不起眼的硬件，请获取专用的 WiFi 接入点，并将它们放置在整个建筑物中。有很多类似大型烟雾探测器的形状。

如果您想要单个设备，还有很多垃圾桶形状的家用路由器。

children.author: buildsjets
children.textRawHtml: Why does Wifi 5-7 require designing the case so it is shaped like an F-117 fighter jet instead of a box of candy?
children.textZhHtml: 为什么 Wifi 5-7 需要将外壳设计成 F-117 战斗机的形状，而不是一盒糖果？

children.author: Karliss
children.textRawHtml:
Don't buy one which looks like that if visuals are important for you. I already told that there are plenty of models which avoids this design and form factor. Do I have to spell out specific manufacturers and models?

Almost none of the Ubiquity stuff looks like that. Xiaomi has plenty of white/gray cylinders or boxes with rounded corners. TP-link has whole Deco series, Asus has ZenWifi series. Majority of MikroTik non rack mounted hardware also targets more neutral design.

You also have to consider who is the target audience for dedicated all in one wifi routers. Majority of regular people are fine with the WiFi that's builtin the modem provided by their ISP. Any serious commercial office will have the IT team to setup separate (rack mounted) router/switches and ceiling mounted access points that look like previously mentioned smoke alarms. People with large enough house to need multiple access points but aren't IT specialists willing to wire up Ethernet everywhere -> various product lines described as mesh routers. Like the trash can shaped TP link Deco series and similar from other manufacturers. If your house is not that big, nothing stops you to buy one of them and ignore the mesh functionality. That leaves people living in small enough house/apartment to be served by single router/switch/Wifi access point combo but for some reason not being satisfied what the ISP provides and also wanting multiple wired connections. Exclude the IT specialists willing to set up home lab and you are left with gamers (potentially impressed by black spider) and few others who have hopefully have enough rationality to place the router where it's not an eyesore or picking some of the previously mentioned stuff.

Another factor is move from antennas that are simple correctly size wire maybe with some spiral which easily fits in small rounded antenna to flat pcb antennas which encourage more rectangular design of the antenna housing and rest of the router. A lot of it is still partially just for the show, trying to give the impression "this one has more/bigger antennas must be better WiFi", but oversized partially empty plastic antenna housing were a thing even before current spider trend.

White slightly rounded 8 legged spider still looks like spider. Trash cans have a bunch of antennas but they hide them in larger volume. Dedicated access points have the advantage of being placed more predictably (near ceiling with little obstacles), they also have advantage of being distributed less work for each of them instead of single router covering whole house.
children.textZhHtml:
如果视觉效果对您来说很重要，请不要购买看起来像这样的产品。我已经说过，有很多型号可以避免这种设计和外形尺寸。我必须说出具体的制造商和型号吗？

几乎所有 Ubiquity 的东西都不是这样的。小米有很多白色/灰色的圆柱体或圆角盒子。 TP-link有整个Deco系列，华硕有ZenWifi系列。大多数 MikroTik 非机架安装硬件也以更中性的设计为目标。

您还必须考虑谁是专用全合一 WiFi 路由器的目标受众。 大多数普通人都可以使用 ISP 提供的内置调制解调器的 WiFi。 任何严肃的商业办公室都会让 IT 团队设置独立的（机架安装式）路由器/交换机和吸顶式接入点，类似于前面提到的烟雾报警器。 人们拥有足够大的房子，需要多个接入点，但 IT 专家不愿意在任何地方连接以太网 -> 被称为网状路由器的各种产品线。就像垃圾桶形状的 TP link Deco 系列和其他制造商的类似产品。如果您的房子不是那么大，没有什么可以阻止您购买其中之一并忽略网格功能。 这使得居住在足够小的房屋/公寓中的人们可以通过单个路由器/交换机/Wifi 接入点组合提供服务，但由于某种原因对 ISP 提供的服务不满意，并且还需要多个有线连接。排除愿意建立家庭实验室的IT专家，剩下的就是游戏玩家（可能会被黑蜘蛛打动）和少数其他人，他们希望有足够的理性将路由器放置在不碍眼的地方，或者选择前面提到的一些东西。

另一个因素是从简单的、正确尺寸的电线（可能带有一些螺旋线，可以轻松安装在小型圆形天线中）转向扁平的PCB天线，这鼓励天线外壳和路由器其余部分的更多矩形设计。其中很多仍然只是为了展示，试图给人一种印象“这个有更多/更大的天线一定是更好的 WiFi”，但超大的部分空塑料天线外壳甚至在当前蜘蛛趋势之前就已经是一种现象了。

白色略圆的 8 腿蜘蛛仍然看起来像蜘蛛。垃圾桶有一堆天线，但它们隐藏的体积更大。专用接入点的优点是放置位置更加可预测（靠近天花板，几乎没有障碍物），它们还具有为每个接入点分配较少工作的优点，而不是覆盖整个房子的单个路由器。

children.author: walrus01
children.textRawHtml:
You don't have to buy such a design, the ubiquiti u7 lite and u7 pro are ordinary round white ceiling mount 802.11be AP.

Somehow ordinary non tech consumers got it into their heads that something which looks like a f117 with many spiky antennas sticking out of it must be faster.
children.textZhHtml:
你不必购买这样的设计，ubiquiti u7 lite 和 u7 pro 是普通的圆形白色吊顶安装 802.11be AP。

不知何故，普通非技术消费者认为看起来像 f117 且有许多尖刺天线伸出的东西一定更快。

children.author: radlad
children.textRawHtml:
I could be crazy, but don't all the external antennas allow for more flexibility with regard to directionality of signal?

I have a U7 Lite and it is very directional compared to other routers I have used (spider style, trash can style, etc.)
children.textZhHtml:
可能是我疯了，但所有外置天线难道不是能让信号方向性更具灵活性吗？

我有一台U7 Lite，和我用过的其他路由器（蜘蛛造型、垃圾桶造型等）相比，它的方向性非常强。

children.author: bcrl
children.textRawHtml:
The U7 Lite only does 2x2 MIMO. Compared to 4x4 MIMO in the U6 LR, the U7 Lite therefore does a much poorer job at beamforming (directing the energy of the signal towards the device).

Personally, I find it better to have multiple low end access points (like the TP-Link Archer C80 which has 3x3 MIMO on 5 GHz) deployed to achieve excellent coverage in a house. Sadly, the U7 line is a bit too expensive for that. Plus, I'm loathe to deal with UniFi deployments now that I am well versed in the glass jaws in the platform.

There really is space in the market for a product line that is basically what UniFi is, but done "right". Ie: can be debugged or you can fix it without an internet connection or recover the system when the owner forgets the password and lost access to the email account used for 2FA. UniFi is an absolute nightmare the moment anything goes even slightly wrong.
children.textZhHtml:
U7 Lite仅支持2x2 MIMO。与U6 LR的4x4 MIMO相比，U7 Lite在波束成形（将信号能量定向传输至设备）方面的表现要差得多。

就我个人而言，我觉得部署多个低端接入点（比如支持5GHz频段3x3 MIMO的TP-Link Archer C80）能更好地实现全屋出色覆盖。遗憾的是，U7系列的价格对此来说有点太高了。而且，在彻底了解了该平台存在的致命弱点后，我非常不愿意再折腾UniFi的部署了。

市场上确实存在一个空白，需要一条产品线，它基本上就是UniFi的样子，但能“做对”。也就是说：可以进行调试，或者在没有网络连接的情况下进行修复，或者在机主忘记密码且无法访问用于双重认证的邮箱时恢复系统。UniFi只要出一点点小问题，简直就是场彻头彻尾的噩梦。

children.author: diegs
children.textRawHtml:
I went from Unifi to Ruckus APs and never looked back.

You can find older generations on eBay very inexpensively for what they are, install Ruckus Unleashed firmware on them, and operate them completely locally.

They have the best beamforming antennas in the industry and their firmware is rock solid. I'm on some very old models (2.4ghz and 5ghz only) but they work fantastically well. I'll probably upgrade to some wifi 6 models soon.

But for my uses 500-600mbps rock solid throughout my house is plenty. Anything where I want max throughput/low latency/low jitter (mainly gaming) is hardwired. For wifi I care much more about complete and consistent coverage.
children.textZhHtml:
我从Unifi换成了Ruckus AP，从此再没回头。

你可以在eBay上以极低的价格淘到上一代产品，刷入Ruckus Unleashed固件，就能完全在本地运行。

它们拥有业界顶级的波束成形天线，固件也极其稳定。我现在用的还是些很老的型号（仅支持2.4GHz和5GHz），但表现依然非常出色。我可能很快就会升级到Wi-Fi 6的型号。

但就我的使用需求而言，全屋500-600Mbps的稳定网速已经绰绰有余。凡是需要极限吞吐量、低延迟和低抖动（主要是打游戏）的场景，我都直接插网线。对于Wi-Fi，我更看重的是全面且稳定的覆盖。

children.author: zhengyi13
children.textRawHtml: Yes, perhaps they do allow for greater flexibility, but that's complex and difficult to do well/reliably, and doing it well/reliably requires signal analysis gear and software modeling that's out of the reach of normal consumers.
children.textZhHtml: 是的，也许它们确实能提供更大的灵活性，但这很复杂，很难做好或做到可靠，而要做好或做到可靠，需要普通消费者无法企及的信号分析设备和软件建模。

children.author: numpad0
children.textRawHtml:
Presumably you can do a snare drum shape with antennas arranged like tension rods, for whatever reason they do articulating antennas.

As for why it needs multiple antennas, it's for MIMO and beam forming.
children.textZhHtml:
大概你可以制作一个军鼓形状，天线像拉杆一样排列，无论出于什么原因，它们都需要铰接天线。

至于为什么需要多个天线，是为了 MIMO 和波束成形。

children.author: hwj
children.textRawHtml: I guess the aerodynamic design makes the Wifi faster... ;-)
children.textZhHtml: 我猜空气动力学设计使 Wifi 更快......;-)

children.author: ButlerianJihad
children.textRawHtml: Because the gamers and dads who make all the purchasing decisions about household routers love F-117s, and stealth bombers, and consider fighter jets and black “Nighthawk” branding to be way sexier than a pink box of candy.
children.textZhHtml: 因为做出家用路由器购买决定的游戏玩家和爸爸们喜欢 F-117 和隐形轰炸机，并认为战斗机和黑色“夜鹰”品牌比粉色糖果盒更性感。

children.author: Onavo
children.textRawHtml: A shit ton of beam forming and phased arrays. Why do you think all of a sudden there's a bunch of "WiFi Radar Imaging" projects popping up on HN? It's not just because of advances in ML. Boost the output power by a few more magnitudes and you can probably ship them to Ukraine.
children.textZhHtml: 一堆波束形成和相控阵。您认为为什么突然在 HN 上出现了一堆“WiFi 雷达成像”项目？这不仅仅是因为机器学习的进步。将输出功率再提高几个数量级，说不定就可以运到乌克兰了。

children.author: cromka
children.textRawHtml: That complaint was about its styling, not number of antennas
children.textZhHtml: 该投诉是关于其样式，而不是天线数量

children.author: ButlerianJihad
children.textRawHtml: I am not sure why a simple cylinder shape should be called "trash can shape". It can be favorably compared to many things. How about an R2D2-shaped router?
children.textZhHtml: 我不知道为什么一个简单的圆柱体形状应该被称为“垃圾桶形状”。它可以与许多事物进行比较。 R2D2形状的路由器怎么样？

children.author: vitally3643
children.textRawHtml: The "trash can Mac" made this phrase permanent
children.textZhHtml: “垃圾桶”Mac让这句话成为了经典

children.author: all2
children.textRawHtml:
The WRT54 is still one of my favorite pieces of industrial design. Small case, ~~purple~~blue and grey, two antennas.

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Linksys_WRT54G_series
children.textZhHtml:
WRT54 仍然是我最喜欢的工业设计作品之一。小盒子，~~紫色~~蓝色和灰色，两根天线。

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Linksys_WRT54G_series

children.author: bschwindHN
children.textRawHtml:
My first WiFi router :)

I bought it in anticipation of the Nintendo DS having WiFi capabilities, which I had never heard of before (I was like 13 or 14 then). Had to convince my parents to get broadband internet too.
children.textZhHtml:
我的第一个 WiFi 路由器:)

我购买它是因为预期 Nintendo DS 具有 WiFi 功能，这是我以前从未听说过的（当时我大概 13 岁或 14 岁）。必须说服我的父母也使用宽带互联网。
